title: In O.S.No.1062/2012 vs In O.S.No.1062/2012 on 2 February, 2019
body: In O.S.No.1062/2012 vs In O.S.No.1062/2012 on 2 February, 2019 [C.R.P. 67] Govt. of Karnataka Form No.9 (Civil) Title Sheet for Judgment in Suits (R.P.91) IN THE COURT OF THE XIV ADDL. CITY CIVIL JUDGE AT BANGALORE [CCH.No.28] Present: Sri.G.A.Mulimani, M.A., LL.B. (Spl.) XIV ADDL. CITY CIVIL JUDGE Dated this the 2nd day of February, 2019 O.S.No.1062/2012 C/w O.S.No.1061/2012, O.S. No.1179/2012 & O.S.No.1183/2012, Plaintiff/s in O.S.No.1062/2012: S.Chinnappa, S/o Late Shamanna, Aged about 82 years, No.11/1, Ravi Krupa, 3rd Cross, K.N.Extension, Yeshwanthapura, Bangalore-560022 (By Sri.P.R., Advocate) - Vs - Defendant/s in O.S.No.1062/2012: 1.M/s Jatti Automobiles, No.51, Pattandur Agrahara, Outer Circle, Whitefield, Bangalore-560 066 Rep.by its Proprietor Sri.B.D.Jatti. 2 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 2.M/s Jatti Motors Ltd., No.73, Millers Road, Bangalore-560 052, Rep.by its Director Sri.B.D.Jatti. (By Sri.NSK., Advocate for D1 Def.No.2 - Exparte) Plaintiff/s in O.S.No.1061/2012: S.Chinnappa, S/o Late Shamanna, Aged about 82 years, No.11/1, Ravi Krupa, 3rd Cross, K.N.Extension, Yeshwanthapura, Bangalore-560022 (By Sri.R.D, Advocate) - Vs - Defendant/s in O.S.No.1061/2012: 1. M/s Jatti Automobiles, No.51, Pattandur Agrahara, Outer Circle, Whitefield, Bangalore-560 066 Rep.by its Proprietor Sri.B.D.Jatti. 2. M/s Jatti Motors Ltd., No.73, Millers Road, Bangalore-560 052, Rep.by its Director Sri.B.D.Jatti. 3 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 (By Sri.NSK., Advocate) Plaintiff/s in O.S.No.1179/2012: Smt.Anitha.R. W/o Sri.Prasannakumar, Aged about 37 years, No.11/1, Ravi Krupa, 3rd Cross, K.N.Extension, Yeshwanthapura, Bangalore-560022 (By Sri.N.M.P., Advocate) - Vs - Defendant/s in O.S.No.1179/2012: 1. M/s Jatti Automobiles, No.51, Pattandur Agrahara, Outer Circle, Whitefield, Bangalore-560 066 Rep.by its Proprietor Sri.B.D.Jatti. 2. M/s Jatti Motors Ltd., No.73, Millers Road, Bangalore-560 052, Rep.by its Director Sri.B.D.Jatti. (By Sri.NSK., Advocate for D1 Def.No.2 - Exparte) Plaintiff/s in O.S.No.1183/2012: Smt.C.Gayathri Devi, D/o Chinnappa, 4 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 Aged about 47 years, No.12/2, Mathru Krupa, 3rd Cross, K.N.Extension, Yeshwanthapura, Bangalore-560022 (By Sri.N.M.P., Advocate) - Vs - Defendant/s in O.S.No.1183/2012: M/s Jatti Automobiles, No.51, Pattandur Agrahara, Outer Circle, Whitefield, Bangalore-560 066 Rep.by its Proprietor Sri.B.D.Jatti. (By Sri.NSK., Advocate) Date of institution of the suits : 1. O.S.No.1062/12 - 4.2.2012 2. O.S.No.1061/12 - 4.2.2012 3. O.S.No.1179/12 - 9.2.2012 4. O.S.No.1183/12 - 9.2.2012 Nature of the suit : [suit on pronote, suit for declaration and 1. O.S.No.1062/12 - Recovery of possession, suit for money injunction 2. O.S.No.1061/12 - Recovery of money 3. O.S.No.1179/12 - Recovery of money 4. O.S.No.1183/12 - Recovery of money 5 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 Date of the commencement of recording of the evidence: 03.07.2017 Date on which the Judgment was pronounced : 02.02.2019 Year/s Month/s Day/s Total Duration 1. O.S.1061/2012 06 11 28 2. O.S.1062/2012 06 11 28 3. OS.1179/2012 06 11 23 4. O.S.1183/2012 06 11 23 COMMON JUDGMENT All the above suits are filed by the respective plaintiff against the defendants for recovery of money with interest and such other reliefs. 2. The brief facts of the plaintiff's case in O.S.No.1062/2012 is that: The defendant is a business and proprietor of M/s Jatti Automobiles, on the offer of the defendant, the plaintiff has deposited a sum of Rs.2,00,000/- with an assurance of payment of interest at the rate of 18.33 per annum, the defendant issued deposit receipt bearing No.RV 8719 dated 22.01.2004, the defendant has paid the interest on the deposit only till January, 2009, thereafter, the defendant has not paid any sum either towards principal or towards 6 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 interest till now, inspite of several repeated demands, finally having no option the plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 24.11.2011 by RPAD to the defendant and same is served on the defendant, inspite of service of notice the defendant has neither replied nor has made any payment. Hence, he prayed to decree the suit. The brief facts of the plaintiff's case in O.S.No.1061/2012 is that : The defendant is a business and proprietor of M/s Jatti Automobiles, on the offer of the defendant, the plaintiff has deposited a sum of Rs.2,00,000/- with an assurance of payment of interest at the rate of 18.33 per annum, the defendant issued deposit receipt bearing No.RV 8330 dated 7.01.2004, the defendant has paid the interest on the deposit only till January, 2009, thereafter, the defendant has not paid any sum either towards principal or towards interest till now, inspite of several repeated demands, finally having no option the plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 24.11.2011 by RPAD to the defendant and same is served on the defendant, inspite of service of notice the defendant has neither replied nor has made any payment. Hence, he prayed to decree the suit. 7 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 The brief facts of the plaintiff's case in O.S.No.1079/2012 is that : The defendant is a business and proprietor of M/s Jatti Automobiles, on the offer of the defendant, the plaintiff has deposited a sum of Rs.2,00,000/- with an assurance of payment of interest at the rate of 18.33 per annum, the defendant issued deposit receipt bearing No.RV 8331 dated 7.01.2004, the defendant has paid the interest on the deposit only till January, 2009, thereafter, the defendant has not paid any sum either towards principal or towards interest till now, inspite of several repeated demands, finally having no option the plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 24.11.2011 by RPAD to the defendant and same is served on the defendant, inspite of service of notice the defendant has neither replied nor has made any payment. Hence, he prayed to decree the suit. The brief facts of the plaintiff's case in O.S.No.1183/2012 is that : The defendant is a business and proprietor of M/s Jatti Automobiles, on the offer of the defendant, the plaintiff has deposited a sum of Rs.2,00,000/- with an assurance of payment of interest at the rate of 18.33 per annum, the defendant issued deposit receipt bearing No.RV 8720 dated 22.01.2004, the defendant has paid the interest on the 8 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 deposit only till January, 2009, thereafter, the defendant has not paid any sum either towards principal or towards interest till now, inspite of several repeated demands, finally having no option the plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 24.11.2011 by RPAD to the defendant and same is served on the defendant, inspite of service of notice the defendant has neither replied nor has made any payment. Hence, he prayed to decree the suit. 3. In pursuance of the suit summons, the defendants in O.S.No.1062/2012, O.S.No.1061/2012, OS.No.1179/2012, and O.S.No.1183/2012 appeared through their counsels and filed separate written statements. In their written statements in the above cases they denied the case of the plaintiff, and further contended that the suit of the plaintiff is not complied with the provisions of order XXXVII, as such the plaint has to be dismissed in limine with exemplary cost, the plaintiff has not complied with the provisions of the said order by furnishing the copy of the plaint, IA and copies of the documents relied upon the plaintiff and only a notice has been sent to the defendant, after entering appearance by the defendant the defendant was furnished with the paint copy and IA copy without an affidavit, the above suit does not fall under any of the category of the provisions narrated order 38 rule (2) & 2, further suit is also not maintainable 9 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 for the reasons that there was no written contract between the parties and the plaintiff alleged to have deposited Rs.2,00,000/- with the defendant without any written contract or any agreement, the receipt is not issued by the defendant and further it is also not properly stamped, the receipt is not an instrument in law, hence the plaintiff cannot seek relief under this order, the plaintiff has also not produced any document to show that the defendant has agreed to pay 18.33 % interest per annum the plaintiff has filed this suit with malafide intention to cheat the defendant and also to make unlawful gain out of the transaction which never took place between the plaintiff and the defendant, the deposit receipt shown in the schedule of the plaint are created and concocted for unlawful gain by the plaintiff, hence prayed for dismiss the suits. 4. On the basis of the above pleadings, materials and documents, my predecessor in office has framed the following issues: Issues in OS.No.1062/2012 1. Whether the plaintiff proves that he has deposited a sum of Rs.2,00,000/- with defendant with the assurance of payment of interest at the rate of 18.33% p.a.? 10 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 2. Whether the plaintiff proves that the defendant has committed default in payment of principal and interest on the principal amount? 3. Whether the plaintiff is entitled for the suit claim? 4. What order or decree? Additional Issues: 5. What is the rate of interest to which plaintiff is entitled? 6. Whether the suit is in time? 7. Does the defendant prove that suit is not maintainable as no written contract between the parties? 8. Whether suit of the plaintiff does not comply Order 37 of C.P.C .? Issues in OS.No.1061/2012 1. Whether the plaintiff proves that he has deposited a sum of Rs.2,00,000/- with defendant with the assurance of 11 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 payment of interest at the rate of 18.33% p.a.? 2. Whether the plaintiff proves that the defendant has committed default in payment of principal and interest on the principal amount? 3. Whether the plaintiff is entitled for the suit claim? 4. What order or decree? Additional Issues: 5. What is the rate of interest to which plaintiff is entitled? 6. Whether the suit is in time? 7. Does the defendant prove that suit is not maintainable as no written contract between the parties? 8. Whether suit of the plaintiff does not comply Order 37 of C.P.C .? Issues in OS.No.1179/2012 1. Whether the plaintiff proves that she has deposited a sum of Rs.2,00,000/- with 12 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 defendant with the assurance of payment of interest at the rate of 18.33% p.a.? 2. Whether the plaintiff proves that the defendant has committed default in payment of principal and interest on the principal amount? 3. Whether the plaintiff is entitled for the suit claim? 4. What order or decree? Additional Issues: 5. What is the rate of interest to which plaintiff is entitled? 6. Whether the suit is in time? 7. Does the defendant prove that suit is not maintainable as no written contract between the parties? 8. Whether suit of the plaintiff does not comply Order 37 of C.P.C .? Issues in O.S.No.1183/2012 1. Whether the plaintiff proves that she has deposited a sum of Rs.1,50,000/- with 13 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 defendant with the assurance of payment of interest at the rate of 18.33% p.a.? 2. Whether the plaintiff proves that the defendant has committed default in payment of principal and interest on the principal amount? 3. Whether the plaintiff is entitled for the suit claim? 4. What order or decree? Additional Issues: 5. What is the rate of interest to which plaintiff is entitled? 6. Whether the suit is in time? 7. Does the defendant prove that suit is not maintainable as no written contract between the parties? 8. Whether suit of the plaintiff does not comply Order 37 of C.P.C .? 5. In order to prove the case of the plaintiff, SPA holder of the plaintiff in O.S.No.1061/2012 is examined as P.W.1 got documents marked Ex.P1 to 8 and closed his 14 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 side, and SPA holder of plaintiff in O.S.No.1062/2012 is examined as P.W.1 and got documents marked Ex.P1 to 8, and closed his side. Plaintiff in O.S.No.1179/2012 is examined as P.W.1 and got documents marked Ex.P1 to 5 and closed her side, and plaintiff in OS.No.1183/2012 is examined as P.W.2 and got documents marked Ex.P6 to 8 (a) 6. On the other hand GPA holder of defendant in O.S.No.1062/2012 is examined as D.W.1 and thereafter these cases were clubbed as per the order dated 8.6.2018 and O.S.No.1062/2012 is main case D.W.1 has filed his further chief affidavit in club cases also and got documents marked Ex.D.1 and closed their side, and case is posted for arguments. 7. Heard the arguments on both side. The learned counsel for the plaintiff filed written arguments and relied the following citation. 1. Daya Chand Uttam Prakash Jain And... V/s Santhosh Devi Sharma And filed memo to adopt the written argument filed in O.S.No.1061/2012 to all other connected cases and learned counsel for the defendant has filed written argument in O.S.No.1061/2012 and O.S.No.1062/2012 15 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 8. My answers on the above issues are as follows : In O.S.No.1062/2012 Issue No.1 : Partly in the affirmative, Issue No.2 : In the negative, Issue No.3 : Partly in the affirmative, Issue No.4 : As per final order Additional Issue No.5 : Partly in the affirmative, Additional Issue No.6 : Answer accordingly, Additional Issue No.7 : In the negative, Additional Issue No.8 : In the negative In O.S.No.1061/2012 Issue No.1 : Partly in the affirmative, Issue No.2 : In the negative, Issue No.3 : Partly in the affirmative, Issue No.4 : As per final order Additional Issue No.5 : Partly in the affirmative, Additional Issue No.6 : Answer accordingly, Additional Issue No.7 : In the negative, Additional Issue No.8 : In the negative In O.S.No.1179/2012 Issue No.1 : Partly in the affirmative, Issue No.2 : In the negative, 16 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 Issue No.3 : Partly in the affirmative, Issue No.4 : As per final order Additional Issue No.5 : Partly in the affirmative, Additional Issue No.6 : Answer accordingly, Additional Issue No.7 : In the negative, Additional Issue No.8 : In the negative In O.S.No.1183/2012 Issue No.1 : Partly in the affirmative, Issue No.2 : In the negative, Issue No.3 : Partly in the affirmative, Issue No.4 : As per final order Additional Issue No.5 : Partly in the affirmative, Additional Issue No.6 : Answer accordingly, Additional Issue No.7 : In the negative, Additional Issue No.8 : In the negative REASONS 9. The Learned counsel for the plaintiff filed memo to club these cases together, learned counsel for the defendant submit no objection to allow the memo dt.12.4.2018, hence the said memo was allowed on 8.6.2018, and O.S.No.1179/12, O.S.No.1183/2012, 1061/2012 are clubbed in O.S.No.1062/2012 and O.S.No.1062/2012 is main case, as such after clubbing the 17 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 all the suits the parties are adduced their oral and documentary evidence in O.S.No.1062/2012. 10. ISSUES No.1, 2, Additional Issue No.5 & 7 in O.S.No.1062/2012, O.S.No.1061/2012, O.S.N0.1179/2012, O.S.No.1183/2012 : These issues are interlinked, hence, to avoid the repetition of the same facts, I have discussed these issues simultaneously for my common consideration. 11. It is the specific case of the plaintiff in all the above suits are that, the defendant is a business and proprietor of M/s Jatti Automobiles, on the offer of the defendant, the plaintiff has deposited their respective amounts mentioned in the plaint with an assurance of payment of interest at the rate of 18.33 per annum, the defendant issued deposit receipt mentioned in the plaint schedule, the defendant has paid the interest on the deposit only till January, 2009, thereafter, the defendant has not paid any sum either towards principal or towards interest till now, inspite of several repeated demands and service of legal notice, the defendant has neither replied nor has made any payment. 12. It is the specific case of the defendant in all the above cases are that, the suit of the plaintiff is not complied with the provisions of order XXXVII, as such the plaint has 18 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 to be dismissed in limine with exemplary cost, the above suit does not fall under any of the category of the provisions narrated order 38 rule (2) & 2, further suit is also not maintainable for the reasons that there was no written contract between the parties, the receipt is not issued by the defendant and further it is also not properly stamped, the receipt is not an instrument in law, hence the plaintiff cannot seek relief under this order, the plaintiff has also not produced any document to show that the defendant has agreed to pay 18.33 % interest per annum the plaintiff has filed this suit with malafide intention to cheat the defendant and also to make unlawful gain out of the transaction which never took place between the plaintiff and the defendant, the deposit receipt shown in the schedule of the plaint are created and concocted for unlawful gain by the plaintiff. 13. In order to prove the case of the plaintiffs, SPA holder of the plaintiff in O.S.No.1061/2012 has filed his sworn affidavit in lieu of the chief-examination as P.W.1, wherein he has reiterated the same contents, which he has narrated in his plaint, hence, in order to avoid repetition of the same facts, I have not discussed once again. In support of his oral evidence he has produced 8 documents as Ex.P-1 to Ex.P-8 for the sake of convenience I have summarized these documents as Ex.P-1 is the SPA, Ex.P2 is 19 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 the original receipt, Ex.P-3 is the copy of legal notice, Ex.P- 4 is the postal receipt, Ex.P-5 is the postal acknowledgement, Ex.P-6 is the letter dt.15.07.2009, Ex.P- 7 is the certified copy of annexure to Ex.P-6, Ex.P-8 is the certified copy of the deposition C.C.No.9468/2012. 14. The SPA holder of the plaintiff in O.S.No.1062/2012 has filed his sworn affidavit in lieu of the chief-examination as P.W.1, wherein he has reiterated the same contents, which he has narrated in his plaint, hence, in order to avoid repetition of the same facts, I have not discussed once again. In support of his oral evidence he has produced 8 documents as Ex.P-1 to Ex.P-8 for the sake of convenience I have summarized these documents as Ex.P-1 is the SPA, Ex.P2 is the original receipt, Ex.P-3 is the copy of legal notice, Ex.P-4 is the postal receipt, Ex.P-5 is the postal acknowledgement. Ex.P-6 is the letter dt.15.07.2009, Ex.P-7 is the certified copy of annexure to Ex.P-6, Ex.P-8 is the certified copy of the deposition C.C.No.9468/2012. 15. The plaintiff in O.S.No.1179/2012 and OS.No.1183/2012 have filed their separate sworn affidavits in lieu of the chief-examination as P.W.1 and 2 wherein they have reiterated the same contents, which they have narrated in their plaint, hence, in order to avoid repetition 20 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 of the same facts, I have not discussed once again. In support of their oral evidence they have produced 9 documents as Ex.P-1 to Ex.P-9 for the sake of convenience I have summarized these documents as Ex.P-1 is the Deposit receipt, 7.01.2004, Ex.P2 is the copy of legal notice dt.24.11.2011, Ex.P-3 is the Postal receipt, Ex.P-4 is the C/c of letter, dt.15.07.2009, Ex.P-5 is the certified copy of annexure, Ex.P-6 is the deposit receipt, dt.22.01.2004, Ex.P-7 is the copy of legal notice, dt.24.11.2011, Ex.P-8 & 8(a) are the Postal receipt and acknowledgement, Ex.P-9 is the deposition in C.C.No.9468/2012 16. On the other hand in order to disprove the case of the plaintiff in all the cases, the GPA holder of defendant in O.S.No.1062/2012 has filed his sworn affidavit in lieu of the chief-examination as D.W.1, wherein he has reiterated the same contents, which he has narrated in his written statements, hence, in order to avoid repetition of the same facts, I have not discussed once again. In support of his oral evidence he has produced one document as Ex.D-1 for the sake of convenience, I have summarized these document as Ex.D1 is power of attorney. 17. During the cross examination of P.W.1 in O.S. No.1061/2012 & O.S.No.1062/2012 has stated that, he don't know the date , month of execution of Ex.P1, but it 21 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 was executed in 2007, he executed the same before notary, at that time he accompanied with his father, he has not produced any medical document of his father. 18. The defendant paid interest at the rate of 18.33 % on the deposited amount by way of cash, he has produced the documents pertaining to receiving of interest on 7.1.2004 till 2009, he has received last payment of interest in November 2009, he don't know the exact date. 19. During the cross examination of P.W.1 in O.S.No.1179/2012 has stated that, she came to know that through her uncle Sadashivareddy that the defendants have launched the scheme offering 18.33% interest on deposited amount, she has deposited Rs. 2 lakhs with the defendant by cash and not through demand draft or cheque, she don't know defendant company is a proprietary concerned, any transaction in respect of money is only by way of cheque or demand draft, she don't know any transaction more than Rs.20,000/- is to be made only through cheque, she was not owning the bank account as on 7.1.2004, she was not an income tax accessee as on 7.1.2004, it is false to suggest that, if the defendant would have pay the interest amount the her on her deposit only through cheque only, the defendant has paid last interest to her on 24.1.2009 it is false that she has not deposited any amount with the defendant under the said scheme, the defendant had not 22 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 paid any interest from 2004 to 2009, in order to over look the law of limitation she has deposed falsely that the defendant has paid interest in the year january-2009, she has deposited the amount in Jatti motors, Jatti Automobiles and Jatti Motors are proprietorship concern, and no authorized signatory of the both the concern are not issued the deposit receipt. 20. During the cross examination of P.W.2 in O.S.No.1183/2012 has stated that, she do not remember her income as on 22.12004, she has a bank account, she has no impediment to produce her bank statement as on 22.1.2004, it is true that income tax accessee as on 22.1.2004, she don't know whether she has shown her income tax assessee regarding deposit of Rs.1.5 lakhs with the defendant, witness again stated that she has to verify, the interest on the said deposited amount by the defendant is reflected in her account sometimes, witness voluntarily stated that some she has received by way of cash, she don't know the exact date of last payment by defendant, but it may in the year 2009 Feb. She came to know defendant launched the scheme offering 18.33% interest on the deposited amount through her father, in the year 2004 she had Rs.1.5 lakhs as her father Chinnappa paid the same by way of cash, and the transaction was took place with defendant No.1, she has deposited the amount in Jatti 23 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 motors, Jatti Automobiles and Jatti motors are proprietorship concern, and no authorized signatory of both the concern are not issued the deposit receipt, it is false that she has created Ex.P-1, by taking advantage of computer advancement, it is true that no cheque bounce case are pending in between her and defendants, she don't know the name of a person who has issued Ex.P-1, she has not enquired who is the proprietor of Jatti Motors at the time of depositing the amount. 21. During the cross examination of D.W.1 she has categorically stated that, defendant No.1 is her husband and who is a proprietor, she was not having any role in the Jatti Automobiles, and she don't know the business transaction of the Jatti Automobiles as she was no role in the Jatti Automobiles, her husband is the Chairman of Jatti Motors Pvt., Ltd., she has no official role in the Jatti Motors Pvt.,Ltd. but she is doing accounts monitoring. Further has stated that her husband was examined in C.C.No.9468/2012 before 12th A.C.M.M. Bangalore, it is true that Ex.P-3, 6, 7 were produced by the defendant in C.C.No.9468/2012, but witness voluntarily stated that these documents were no way concerned to this case. 22. It is pertinent to note that, Ex.P-2 and Ex.P-2 in O.S.No.1061/2012 and O.S.No.1062/2012, Ex.P1 and Ex.P6 24 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 in O.S.No.1179/2012 and O.S.No.1183/2012 are receipts issued by the Authorized signatory for Jatti motors Pvt.Ltd., dt.7.1.2004 and 22.1.2004, respectively, regarding received a sum of Rs.2,00,000/-, each and also mentioned that received with thanks from Mr/Miss. Chinnappa,S, and Anitha, and Gayathridevi.C. Ex.P-3 in O.S.No. 1061/12 and OS.No.1062/2012, Ex.P2 and 7 in O.S.No.1179/2012 and O.S.No.1183/2012 are the copies of the legal notice issued by the plaintiff's counsel against Jatti Automobiles its prop: B.D.Jatti through RPAD. Wherein calling upon the defendant to pay a sum of Rs.2 lakh with the interest at the rate of 18.33% p.a., Ex.P-6 in O.S.No.1061/12 and OS.No.1062/12, Ex.P-4 O.S.No.1179/2012 and O.S.No.1183/2012 are the letters issued by B.D.Jatti from Jatti products dt.15th July 2009, wherein it is clearly mentioned that being in the business of automobiles and reality sectors got the maximum negative impact due to the global economic recession. Jatti motor Pvt. Limited once was the leader and pioneer in Automobile Industry has suffered a huge loss to the tune of Rs.11.5 crores etc., further has contended that, I assure that, I will return the principal deposit amount only........,. Ex.P-7 in O.S.No.1061/12 and OS.No.1062/12, Ex.P-5 in O.S.No.1179/2012 and O.S.No.1183/2012 are the annexure which shows the name of the account, amount and its serial number. Sl.No.2 denotes Chinnappa, S. 25 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 amount shown is Rs.17,00,000/-, and shown the name of the account, amount and its serial number. Sl.No.1 denotes Anitha, B.R. amount shown is Rs.4,50,000/-, and shows the name of the account, amount and its serial number. Sl.No.3 denotes Gayathridevi.C. amount shown is Rs.4,50,000/-,Ex.P-8 is the deposition of B.D.Jatti in C.C.No.9468/2012 in O.S.No.1061/2012. 23. However, though the D.W.1 has denied the relationship between the defendant and Jatti Motors and she has denied each and every transactions of the plaintiff, and also suggested by the learned counsel for the defendant in the cross examination of P.W.2 that he has created the Ex.P-2. The Ex.P-6 in O.S.No.1061/12 and OS.No.1062/12 and Ex.P-4 in O.S.No.1179/2012 and 1183/12 are the letter written by the D.B.Jatti, Jatti Projects Inc. Bangalore dated 15th July 2009, wherein he has clearly mentioned that, due to the global economic recession the Jatti Motors Pvt.Ltd. Industry has suffered a huge loss tune of Rs.11.5 crores, and also assured to the plaintiff Chinnappa by the said letter that he will return the principle deposit amount only etc., on taking into consideration of the recitals of the Ex.P-6 in O.S.No.1061/12 and OS.No.1062/12 and Ex.P-4 in O.S.No.1179/2012 and 1183/12, since the very proprietor of the Jatti Projects is admits that Jatti Motors Pvt.Ltd., is 26 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 also a sister concern of Jatti Projects Inc. taking into consideration of the above oral and documentary evidence, though the D.W.1 has denied the entire transaction of the plaintiff with the defendant, and also denied the Ex.P-2 in O.S.No.1061/12 and OS.No.1062/12 receipt dt.7.1.2004, but the Ex.P-6 in O.S.No.1061/12 and OS.No.1062/12 and Ex.P-1 in O.S.No.1179/2012 and Ex.P-6 in O.S.No.1183/2012 are a document which corroborate the claim of the plaintiff, as such the suggestion suggested by the learned counsel for the defendant to the P.W.2 that Ex.P-2 is created by him is not accepted, and in view of the denial of the plaintiff by the defendant the defendant is liable to pay the said amount with interest. 24. So for as interest is concerned, P.W.1 has stated in his cross examination Ex.P-2 was issued by Jatti Motors , it is true that Ex.P-2 does not disclose the clause pertains to rate of interest at 18.33% p.a. It is true that except Ex.P-2 no documents issued by the defendant regarding payment of interest at the rate of 18.33 % p.a. Ex.P-2 sends by office bearers, he don't know the defendant company is a partnership concern, no authorized signatory are appointed to execute Ex.P-2. Though there is no written contract between the parties, on the basis of the Ex.P-2 and 6 in O.S.No.1061/12 and OS.No.1062/12 Ex.P-1 and Ex.P6 in O.S.No.1179/2012 and 1183/12 the defendants are liable 27 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 to pay the amount, admittedly the said transaction is a commercial transaction, in the absence of written contract also the interest can be awarded in judiciously, taking into consideration of the above , it is just, fair and reasonable to award the interest at the rate of 8% per annum from the date of the suit till its realization nor harm or prejudice will be caused to the other side. On the basis of the above discussion and foregoing reasons, I am of the opinion that, plaintiff proves that he deposited a sum of Rs.2 lakhs in the defendants, and the defendants have failed to pay the said amount with interest, as such the defendants are liable to pay 8% per annum from the date of the suit till its realization. Hence, I answer the issue No.1 in O.S.No.1062/2012, O.S.No.1061/2012, O.S.No.1179/2012 and O.S.No.1183/2012 partly in the affirmative, issue No.2 in the said suits answer in the negative, and Additional issue No.5 in the said suits answer partly in the affirmative, and additional issue No.7 in the said suits answer in the negative. 25. Additional Issue No.6, 8 and issue No.3 in O.S.No.1062/2012, OS.No.1061/2012, O.S.No.1179/2012, O.S.No.1183/2012:- On taking into consideration of the entire evidence of the defendant, and pleadings, the defendant has not pleaded which and what manner the suit of the plaintiff was barred by limitation, mere taking 28 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 contention in the suit that the suit of the plaintiff is not maintainable as it is barred by limitation is not acceptable, since the plaintiff got issued notice through their Advocate on 24.11.2011 and this suit is filed on 4.2.2012 thereby this suit is well within the time, the learned counsel for the defendant has contended that the plaintiff has complied the provisos under Order 37 of C.P.C ., however this suit was filed in the year 2012 till the date of assuming charge by me of this court none of the parties have not taken any steps or try to comply the said order, after recording all the stages when the case is come up for final stage has argued that the plaintiff has not complied the order 37 of C.P.C ., however even though in a suit institute under Order 37 of C.P.C . if the defendant sought leave to contest the suit raising triable issue, the court should not decline the grant of leave, since the defendant has contested the matter and there are triable issue, if there are triable issues notice to the court the court may impose condition regarding time and mode of trial, therefore, the contention taken by the defendants is not survival in this stage, as and when the triable issues were framed it is presumed to modified the trial, therefore which is not fatal, under these circumstances the plaintiff is entitled the relief in part as prayed. Hence, I answer additional issue No. 8 in O.S.No.1062/2012, O.S.No.1061/2012, O.S.No.1179/2012 and O.S.No.1183/2012 are in the negative and additional issue 29 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/ 12 , OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 No.6 answer accordingly, and issue No.3 in the said suits answer partly in the affirmative. 26. ISSUE No.4 in O.S.No.1062/2012, O.S.No.1061/2012, O.S.No.1179/2012 and O.S.No.1183/2012 : In the result, I proceed to pass the following: ORDER The suit of the plaintiff in O.S.No.1062/2012 is hereby decreed in part with costs. The defendants are liable to pay a sum of Rs.3,09,980/-with interest at the rate of 8% per annum from the date of the suit till its realization. The Suit of the plaintiff in O.S.No.1061/2012 is hereby decreed in part with costs. The defendants are liable to pay a sum of Rs.3,09,980/-with interest at the rate of 8% per annum from the date of the suit till its realization. 30 O.S.No.1062/12 C/w O.S.1061/12, OS.1179/12, OS.1183/12 The Suit of the plaintiff in O.S.No.1179/2012 is hereby decreed in part with costs. The defendants are liable to pay a sum of Rs.3,09,980/-with interest at the rate of 8% per annum from the date of the suit till its realization. The Suit of the plaintiff in O.S.No.1183/2012 is hereby decreed in part with costs. The defendant is liable to pay a sum of Rs.2,32,485/-with interest at the rate of 8% per annum from the date of the suit till its realization. Draw the decree accordingly in all the cases. Keep copies of this judgment in O.S.No.1061/2012, O.S.No.1179/2012, and O.S.No.1183/2012. (Dictated to the judgment writer directly on computer, corrected by me and then pronounced by me in the open court, on this 2nd day of February, 2019). (G.A.MULIMANI) XIV Addl.
